questions.q: Which collection on a set $X$ is always a $\sigma$-algebra?
choices: All finite subsets of an infinite $X$
questions.explain: $\{\varnothing, X\}$ is closed under complements and countable unions. Open sets and intervals are not closed under complements, and finite subsets are not closed under countable unions in an infinite set.

questions.q: Let $\mu$ be a measure with $\mu(A)=3$, $\mu(B)=4$, and $\mu(A\cap B)=1$. Compute $\mu(A\cup B)$.
questions.explain: Use finite additivity with overlap correction: $\mu(A\cup B)=\mu(A)+\mu(B)-\mu(A\cap B)=3+4-1=6$.

questions.q: What is the Lebesgue measure of a singleton $\{x\}\subset\mathbb{R}$?
questions.explain: Points have zero length, so every singleton has Lebesgue measure $0$.

questions.q: Compute $m([2,5])$, where $m$ is Lebesgue measure on $\mathbb{R}$.
questions.explain: Intervals are measured by length: $m([a,b])=b-a$, so $m([2,5])=5-2=3$.
$(1+i)^2=1+2i+i^2=2i$, so the real part is $0$ and imaginary part is $2$.

questions.q: A function $f\colon(X,\mathcal A)\to(\mathbb R,\mathcal B)$ is measurable iff for every Borel set $B\subset\mathbb R$ we have:
questions.explain: Measurability is defined by preimages: all Borel sets pull back to measurable sets in $\mathcal A$.

questions.q: Let $f=\mathbf 1_{[0,2]}$ on $\mathbb R$. What is $\int f\,dm$?
questions.explain: The indicator integral equals measure of the set: $\int \mathbf 1_E\,dm = m(E)$, so $m([0,2])=2$.
Complex conjugation flips the sign of the imaginary part: $\overline{a+bi}=a-bi$.

questions.q: For nonnegative measurable $f$, the Lebesgue integral is defined as:
choices: an infimum over upper Riemann sums
a supremum of integrals of simple functions $s\le f$
the limit of midpoint sums only
questions.explain: For $f\ge 0$, $\int f\,d\mu := \sup\{\int s\,d\mu : 0\le s\le f,\ s\text{ simple}\}$.
Lebesgue and Riemann integrals agree here: $\int_0^1 x\,dx = [x^2/2]_0^1 = 1/2$.
$e^{ix}=\cos x+i\sin x$. Both $\int_0^{2\pi}\cos x\,dx$ and $\int_0^{2\pi}\sin x\,dx$ are $0$, so the complex integral is $0+0i$.

questions.q: If $0\le f_n\uparrow f$ pointwise, which theorem gives $\int f_n\to\int f$?
questions.explain: MCT exactly states: for increasing nonnegative measurable $f_n$, integrals converge upward to the integral of the limit.

questions.q: Let $f_n(x)=x^n$ on $[0,1]$. Compute $\int_0^1 f_n(x)\,dx$ in terms of $n$ at $n=9$.
questions.explain: $\int_0^1 x^n\,dx = 1/(n+1)$, so for $n=9$ the value is $1/10=0.1$.

questions.q: Which statement is true on a finite-measure space?
questions.explain: By Hölder, on finite measure spaces one has $\|f\|_1\le \mu(X)^{1/2}\|f\|_2$, so $L^2\subset L^1$.
